---
title: "Tidy Tuesday: Coastal Ocean Temperature by Depth"
author: "Sean Thimons"
date: "2026-04-02"
description: "Exploring vertical temperature profiles in Lunenburg County's coastal waters."
categories:
  - tidytuesday
  - R
  - oceanography
  - climate
---
    11|
    12|```{r}
    13|#| label: pseudo-hero-palette
    14|#| echo: false
    15|#| message: false
    16|#| warning: false
    17|#| fig-width: 10
    18|#| fig-height: 1.35
    19|#| out-width: 100%
    20|
    21|if (!requireNamespace("pak", quietly = TRUE)) {
    22|  install.packages("pak", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
    23|}
    24|
    25|for (pkg in c("ggplot2", "paletteer", "tibble")) {
    26|  if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
    27|    pak::pkg_install(pkg)
    28|  }
    29|}
    30|
    31|library(ggplot2)
    32|library(paletteer)
    33|library(tibble)
    34|
    35|palette_name   <- "oslo"
    36|palette_source <- "scico"
    37|palette_type   <- "continuous"
    38|palette_id     <- paste0(palette_source, "::", palette_name)
    39|
    40|hero_swatches <- if (palette_type %in% c("continuous", "diverging")) {
    41|  as.character(paletteer::paletteer_c(palette_id, n = 5))
    42|} else {
    43|  rep_len(as.character(paletteer::paletteer_d(palette_id, n = 5)), 5)
    44|}
    45|
    46|swatch_df <- tibble(
    47|  idx  = seq_along(hero_swatches),
    48|  xmin = seq_along(hero_swatches) - 1,
    49|  xmax = seq_along(hero_swatches),
    50|  fill = hero_swatches
    51|)
    52|
    53|p_hero <- ggplot(swatch_df) +
    54|  geom_rect(
    55|    aes(xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax, ymin = 0, ymax = 1, fill = fill),
    56|    color = NA,
    57|    show.legend = FALSE
    58|  ) +
    59|  scale_fill_identity() +
    60|  annotate(
    61|    "text",
    62|    x = 0,
    63|    y = -0.22,
    64|    hjust = 0,
    65|    label = paste0("Palette: ", palette_name),
    66|    fontface = "bold",
    67|    size = 4.1
    68|  ) +
    69|  annotate(
    70|    "text",
    71|    x = 5,
    72|    y = -0.22,
    73|    hjust = 1,
    74|    label = paste0("Source: ", palette_source),
    75|    color = "grey35",
    76|    size = 3.6
    77|  ) +
    78|  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 5), ylim = c(-0.35, 1), clip = "off", expand = FALSE) +
    79|  theme_void() +
    80|  theme(plot.margin = margin(5.5, 0, 18, 0))
    81|
    82|p_hero
    83|```
    84|
    85|# Preface
    86|
    87|From [TidyTuesday repository](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/blob/main/data/2026/2026-03-31/readme.md).
    88|
    89|> This dataset explores the complex relationship between ocean depth and mean water temperature in coastal monitoring sites.
    90|
    91|## Loading necessary packages
    92|
    93|My handy booster pack that allows me to install (if needed) and load my usual and favorite packages, as well as some helpful functions.
    94|
    95|```{r}
    96|#| label: booster-pack
    97|#| message: false
    98|#| warning: false
    99|#| code-fold: true
   100|
   101|# Packages ----------------------------------------------------------------
   102|
   103|{
   104|  # Install pak if it's not already installed
   105|  if (!requireNamespace("pak", quietly = TRUE)) {
   106|    install.packages("pak", repos = "https://cloud.r-project.org")
   107|  }
   108|
   109|  # CRAN Packages ----
   110|  install_booster_pack <- function(package, load = TRUE) {
   111|    for (pkg in package) {
   112|      if (!requireNamespace(pkg, quietly = TRUE)) {
   113|        pak::pkg_install(pkg)
   114|      }
   115|      if (load) {
   116|        library(pkg, character.only = TRUE)
   117|      }
   118|    }
   119|  }
   120|
   121|  core_packages <- c(
   122|    'fs',
   123|    'here',
   124|    'janitor',
   125|    'rio',
   126|    'tidyverse',
   127|    'skimr',
   128|    'paletteer',
   129|    'tidytuesdayR'
   130|  )
   131|
   132|  optional_packages <- c('gghighlight', 'ggthemes', 'patchwork')
   133|
   134|  install_booster_pack(package = unique(c(core_packages, optional_packages)), load = TRUE)
   135|  rm(install_booster_pack, core_packages, optional_packages)
   136|
   137|  # Custom Functions ----
   138|
   139|  `%ni%` <- Negate(`%in%`)
   140|
   141|  geometric_mean <- function(x) {
   142|    exp(mean(log(x[x > 0]), na.rm = TRUE))
   143|  }
   144|
   145|  my_skim <- skim_with(
   146|    numeric = sfl(
   147|      n = length,
   148|      min = ~ min(.x, na.rm = T),
   149|      p25 = ~ stats::quantile(., probs = .25, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE),
   150|      med = ~ median(.x, na.rm = T),
   151|      p75 = ~ stats::quantile(., probs = .75, na.rm = TRUE, names = FALSE),
   152|      max = ~ max(.x, na.rm = T),
   153|      mean = ~ mean(.x, na.rm = T),
   154|      geo_mean = ~ geometric_mean(.x),
   155|      sd = ~ stats::sd(., na.rm = TRUE),
   156|      hist = ~ inline_hist(., 5)
   157|    ),
   158|    append = FALSE
   159|  )
   160|}
   161|
   162|
   163|```
   164|
   165|# Load raw data from package
   166|
   167|```{r}
   168|#| label: raw-data-load
   169|#| echo: true
   170|#| output: false
   171|
   172|raw <- tidytuesdayR::tt_load('2026-03-31')
   173|
   174|ocean_temp <- raw$ocean_temperature
deployments <- raw$ocean_temperature_deployments
   175|
   176|```
   177|
   178|# Exploratory Data Analysis
   179|
   180|The `my_skim()` function is a modified version of the `skimr::skim()` function that returns the number of missing data points (cells as `NA`) as well as the inverse (e.g.: number of rows that are *not* `NA`), the count, minimum, 25%, median, 75%, max, mean, geometric mean, and standard deviation. It also generates a little ASCII histogram. Neat!
   181|
   182|## Ocean Temperature Data
   183|
   184|```{r}
   185|#| label: eda-ocean-temp
   186|#| echo: true
   187|#| output: true
   188|
   189|my_skim(ocean_temp)
   190|
   191|```
   192|
   193|The dataset contains vertical temperature measurements across multiple depths. We see a clear depth-temperature gradient.
   194|
   195|# Seasonal Stability vs Depth
   196|
   197|How does the variance (standard deviation) of temperature behave as we go deeper? We might expect deeper waters to be more thermally stable throughout the year.
   198|
   199|```{r}
   200|#| label: depth-stability-plot
   201|#| message: false
   202|#| warning: false
   203|#| fig.width: 10
   204|#| fig.height: 8
   205|
   206|
p <- ocean_temp %>%
  mutate(month = month(date, label = TRUE)) %>%
  group_by(sensor_depth_at_low_tide_m, month) %>%
  summarize(
    mean_temp = mean(mean_temperature_degree_c, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_temp = mean(sd_temperature_degree_c, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = month, y = mean_temp, group = factor(sensor_depth_at_low_tide_m))) +
  geom_line(aes(color = sensor_depth_at_low_tide_m), linewidth = 1.2) +
  scale_color_paletteer_c("scico::oslo", direction = -1) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Ocean Temperature Cycles by Sensor Depth",
    subtitle = "Deepest sensors (darker) show more stability and slightly warmer winter mins",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Mean Temperature (°C)",
    color = "Depth (m)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )
   207|
   208|# IMPORTANT: The last line of this chunk MUST be the plot object name
   209|# so that Quarto renders it. Never end with an assignment.
   210|p
   211|
   212|```
   213|
   214|# Final thoughts and takeaways
   215|
   216|The data confirms that while the surface waters experience the most extreme swings, the deeper strata provide a more stable thermal environment, which is critical for local sea life during the transition seasons.
   217|
